notes: 29 issues triaged for anomaly relevance; 14 with actionable root_cause+fix included below. Skipped: pure UI/doc bugs, unresolved tickets without verified fix, code-comprehension questions.

schema.title: issue title (translated where needed)
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}
schema.version: issue's reported version + fix version (when known)

tuples.title: 1.2.2 WAL accumulation after JVM hang/restart eventually rejects writes
tuples.symptom: After JVM hung-restart, WAL files keep accumulating; manual FLUSH only frees 2 of 3 DataNodes; one DataNode never rotates; writes eventually rejected with WAL throttle
tuples.root_cause: 1.2.2 has WAL cleanup bug where MemTable→WAL accounting drifts after abrupt restart; the WAL position pointer may stay above accumulated files indefinitely
fix: Upgrade to 1.3.3+ — newer versions reportedly fix this WAL accounting bug
Do NOT delete WAL files by mtime — risks data loss because each WAL maps to MemTables that may not have flushed
Workaround pre-upgrade: FLUSH ON CLUSTER then restart; check WAL count drops
Monitor wal file_count breakdown per node to detect drift early

tuples.title: Pipe (TsFile sync) loses ~800/96000 data points under heavy insert load (1.1.1)
tuples.symptom: Cloud pipe receiver missing TsFiles; edge node logs `Waiting for tsfile <name> close, retry 10/10` and `FileNotFoundException: ...tsfile.resource, maybe the tsfile is not closed yet`
tuples.root_cause: 1.1.1 pipe-sync module had race between TsFile sealing and resource-file generation under high write rate; sealed TsFile not yet ready when sync scanner picks it up
fix: Upgrade to 1.2.2+ — sync module rebuilt in 1.2
Use the new Data-Sync pipe (1.2.x docs) instead of legacy pipe-sink

tuples.title: 0.13.2 endless 'Running compaction task failed' on missing .cross temp files
tuples.symptom: Continuous error: `[Compaction] compaction for target file [file is .../1699280358127-1-0-1.cross, status: UNCLOSED]`; file does not actually exist on disk; queries report slow log
tuples.root_cause: Compaction recorded a target .cross temp file in memory state but failed mid-flight; when recovery scans, the file is gone (it was supposed to be deleted on failure) but state still references it — task keeps retrying
fix: Manually run FLUSH then MERGE — clears stale compaction state
Upgrade if possible (>=1.0 likely fixed); 0.13.x unmaintained
Inspect log_datanode_all.log for `CompactionExceptionHandler` to see root failure that led to orphan .cross
Note: path with digits broke direct upgrade for this user — backup and fresh install if upgrade blocked

tuples.title: FileReaderManager leaks file streams after compaction (deleted files held open)
tuples.symptom: After compactions complete, the deleted source TsFiles still hold open file descriptors; `lsof` shows growing list of (deleted) entries; may eventually hit 'too many open files'
tuples.root_cause: FileReaderManager caches readers per file; on compaction the source file is deleted but the cached reader is not invalidated/closed
fix: Upgrade to 1.3.x+ — fixed by PR https://github.com/apache/iotdb/pull/10688
Workaround: restart DataNode periodically to release held descriptors
Raise ulimit -n 65535 (see TS-HC-2) to delay symptom

tuples.title: export-tsfile.sh fails with `301: not enough memory` on whole-DB query
tuples.symptom: `Cannot dump result because: 301: There is not enough memory to execute current fragment instance, current remaining free memory is 86MB, estimated memory usage 257MB`
tuples.root_cause: Default 4G heap leaves only 4G × datanode_memory_proportion(30%) × chunk_timeseriesmeta_free_memory_proportion(20%) ≈ 240MB for query execution; SELECT * over many series exceeds it
fix: Increase IOTDB_HEAP_SIZE to >4.2G so 4.2G × 30% × 20% > 257MB
Or raise datanode_memory_proportion (more heap → queries)
Or reduce timeseries in query (slimit N)
Or add ALIGN BY DEVICE to bound memory to per-device level
Or split export into multiple smaller queries

tuples.title: Docker: MEMORY_SIZE in iotdb-env.sh ignored → OOM during compaction recovery
tuples.symptom: Standalone Docker IoTDB 1.3.2/2.0.5 ignores MEMORY_SIZE=4G in env; at startup `recovering sequence inner compaction` keeps growing container memory until OOM; RPC port 6667 never opens
tuples.root_cause: iotdb-env.sh MEMORY_SIZE assignments are not propagated inside the container; Docker entry-point reads JVM flags from environment variables instead
fix: Set JVM heap via docker-compose environment variables:
- IOTDB_JMX_OPTS=-Xms4G -Xmx4G -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=1G
- CONFIGNODE_JMX_OPTS=-Xms1G -Xmx1G -XX:MaxDirectMemorySize=256M
Verify with: docker exec <c> jmap -heap <pid>
Setting docker-compose memory: 6G limit alone doesn't help — need JVM-level caps

tuples.title: load tsfile with onSuccess=none fails with `Status code: 700` and `301 null`
tuples.symptom: LOAD '/path/to.tsfile' onSuccess=none verify=false → executeStatement failed status 700; subsequent retries cascade into `501 root.ss has already` and `301 null` memory errors
tuples.root_cause: Likely combo: large TsFile load attempts to allocate memory above limit (error 301); failed metadata partial-commit blocks subsequent create-database (501)
fix: Restart DataNode to clear partial state, then retry LOAD with smaller TsFiles
Verify heap headroom before LOAD (see ISS-11967 memory tuning)
If verify=false caused metadata mismatch: use verify=true on next LOAD

tuples.title: Cluster insert with batchSize=10000 throws `RatisRequestFailedException: Ratis request failed`
tuples.symptom: Insert Tablet with rowCount=10000 → `605: RatisRequestFailedException`; same code with rowCount=1000 succeeds; problem appears near completion of 3.77M-row batch insert
tuples.root_cause: Ratis consensus restricts single write request to ≤4MB; batchSize=10000 with 30 fields × 100 measurements produces ~6MB per request, exceeding limit
fix: Raise data_region_ratis_log_appender_buffer_size_max to 16777216 (16MB) in iotdb-common.properties on ConfigNode
Restart cluster
Or reduce client batch size to keep request <4MB
Estimate per-request size: rowCount × fields × avg_value_size + overhead

tuples.title: Docker: ConfigNodeClient `The current node may have been down TEndPoint(ip:iotdb)`
tuples.symptom: Standalone Docker startup logs repeat `ConfigNodeClient:255 - The current node may have been down TEndPoint(ip:iotdb, port:10710), try next node`
tuples.root_cause: Hostname `iotdb` used in cn_target_config_node_list is the docker service name, not the in-container hostname; container resolves `iotdb` to itself but networking expects the docker service alias
fix: Rename docker-compose service to something other than `iotdb` (e.g. `iotdb-service`)
Ensure cn_internal_address / dn_internal_address / cn_target_config_node_list / dn_target_config_node_list use this hostname consistently
Verify with `docker exec <container> ping iotdb-service`

tuples.title: destroy-datanode.sh destroyed wrong node, cluster down; need to recover TsFiles into new cluster
tuples.symptom: After running `destroy-datanode.sh -h <failed_node>` from a healthy node, the executing node ALSO went offline; cluster cannot start; tried to LOAD original sequence/unsequence dir into fresh cluster but errors
tuples.root_cause: destroy-datanode is destructive and clears node state; running it from another node may leave both in inconsistent partition state
fix: Recovery: build fresh cluster, then LOAD TsFiles — but do NOT place TsFiles inside the new cluster's iotdb data directory; put them in an external path and LOAD from there

destroy-datanode removes the targeted node from partition table; the originating node is not supposed to be affected, but in 1.3.2 this caused cascade — recommend taking backup before any destroy command
For HA: never run destroy from a 3-node-just-lost-1 cluster without quorum verification first

tuples.title: Windows: `TThreadPoolServer$WorkerProcess - Connection reset` then CLI/workbench can't connect; MQTT receives but stops writing
tuples.symptom: Sudden error in logs: `TTransportException: java.net.SocketException: Connection reset`; CLI hangs, JDBC fails; MQTT keeps receiving but data not persisted; REST API still works for reads
tuples.root_cause: On Windows cmd window, mouse click triggers QuickEdit-mode pause — entire IoTDB process freezes; existing TCP connections reset by OS while process suspended; MQTT (separate listener) may keep accepting but write path blocked
fix: Disable QuickEdit mode on cmd window: right-click title bar → Properties → Options → uncheck Quick Edit Mode
Better: run IoTDB as a Windows service (sbin\windows\install-datanode.bat)
Or run via PowerShell which does not have this pause behaviour
For Linux: run with nohup or systemd to avoid TTY pause issues

tuples.title: 1.0 'ORDER BY time DESC LIMIT N' times out for N>1 on 1M-row query
tuples.symptom: `select * from root... order by time desc limit 1` works (~50ms); same query with `limit 2` (or more) → `301: FragmentInstance ... is aborted by timeout`; only ASC order works
tuples.root_cause: 1.0 desc query plan likely scans full series in reverse without proper TsFile metadata pruning when N>1 (descending iterator costs O(N) chunk loads in worst case)
fix: Upgrade past 1.0 (later versions added desc scan optimization)
Workaround: SELECT * FROM x WHERE time > <past_threshold> ORDER BY time DESC LIMIT N to give the planner a time-pruning hint
Or use multi-step: get max time via separate query, then range query backwards

tuples.title: Docker on Kylin/Rocky Linux hangs at 'Checking whether the ports are already occupied'
tuples.symptom: IoTDB Docker startup blocks at the port-check step; no progress; no error; affects Kylin OS and Rocky Linux distros
tuples.root_cause: Startup script uses nc/lsof for port checks; on minimal OS images these tools missing or block on certain kernel configs
fix: Install nc and lsof in the base image before starting IoTDB
If using prebuilt image: rebuild with `RUN yum install -y nc lsof` (Rocky) or apt-get equivalent for Kylin variants
Skip the port check by running the startup script with `-skip-port-check` (if available) or comment out the check section

tuples.title: [Open/recurring] Partition Bug 2.0.5/2.0.6 — SELECT with time range returns empty even though data exists
tuples.symptom: Same SELECT with explicit time-range WHERE returns empty; widening the WHERE returns data; problem reproducible across 2.0.5 and 2.0.6
tuples.root_cause: Suspected partition routing bug — query plan may dispatch to wrong DataRegion for the time range; not yet root-caused upstream
fix: Status: actively triaged by maintainer @CRZbulabula; no merged fix yet (as of 2026-05)
Workaround 1: omit time WHERE and post-filter client-side
Workaround 2: rephrase as `time >= start - 1ms AND time < end + 1ms`
Workaround 3: rollback to 2.0.4 if not too disruptive
